topic: Section 4.1 — The 28-Domain / 100% Dashboard: One Ruler, Many Rooms (EXP-176 integration, 28-domain rewrite)
change_summary: Decider integrated EXP-176 replacing the entire <details id='p4-41-0'> block for Part 4.1. The pre-EXP-176 version ('9.2-Sigma Dashboard — One Constant, Not Twenty Tests', 1,724 words) has been replaced by a 2,376-word 28-domain classification: a new TLDR, four framing paragraphs (including λg-fitted-from-three-points argument and the 'one ruler, many rooms' analogy), and five classification tables — Group A (14 λg-dependent), Group B (14 static re-references to prior WINs, NEW), Group C (known-constants imports), Group D (2 Selleri/Lorentz recastings), Group E (3 loose checks). Adds cross-reference to SEC-2b.5 for per-domain audit. Quotes methodology.json 'display ratio is bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit' as the audit invitation. Preserves template vars {{CA_REVIEWED}} and {{CA_DOME}} in the closing paragraph. Block length: 18,959 chars; 1 <details> open, 1 close (matches original nesting).
our_argument_summary: The section audits the dome's April 2026 dashboard expansion from 20 to 28 scored domains at a claimed '100% / 9.2σ aggregate significance.' The core argument: 14 cells are λg-dependent (share a single fitted constant λg = 8,619 km derived from a two-parameter exponential fitted to three Schumann/Polaris/H-parameterization data points), 14 are static re-references to prior WINs (monitor.py checks predicted==observed where both sides are identical hardcoded literals), plus known-constant imports (sidereal day, WGS84 gravity, ΛCDM Hubble) and framework recastings (Selleri/Lorentz equivalence). After classification, the 28/28 passing headline reduces to roughly 1 genuinely-discriminating cell, and the 9.2σ aggregate collapses from 'extraordinary convergence' to 'many labels wrapped around the same handful of knobs.' The section credits methodology.json's own 'display ratio is bookkeeping' concession and frames the audit as the author's invitation.

tldr_review.notes: TLDR correctly compresses the 28-domain argument: '14 static re-references, five globe/standard-model imports, two framework recastings, same handful of fitted constants.' One numerical mismatch: TLDR says 'five import globe/standard-model values' but Group C table has six rows (K1, S2, M2, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift, P-wave Shadow Zone). Either the TLDR should say six, or Group C should drop one row (Equatorial Gravity is arguably double-counted since it's also Group A row 4). Severity: minor — the argument doesn't depend on 5 vs 6, but a careful reader will notice.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt (2026-04-07 archive — lists 20 named domains and a 28/28 snapshot header)
https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/formula_runtime.json (referenced by section, not re-fetched this review)
https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html (referenced by section, not re-fetched this review)

data/wins.json (71 entries; confirms WIN-024, WIN-039, WIN-001 exist but WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076 do NOT exist in our registry)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section-level prose. Quantitative claims: (a) 28 scored domains, (b) 100.0% passing, (c) 9.2σ / p=1.2×10⁻²⁰, (d) 14+14 partition of 28 into λg-dependent vs static re-references, (e) roughly 10 independent numerical knobs from 3 master geometric constants + 6 formulas, (f) λg=8,619 km fitted to 3 data points (two-parameter exponential, 1 residual DoF), (g) WIN-024, WIN-039, WIN-001 each appear twice in the 28.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The section correctly frames the dome's own claim structure: monitor.py hardcodes 'predicted' and 'observed' as identical literals in the 14 static cells (pred==obs by construction, tolerance irrelevant). The section's strongest contribution is naming this pattern publicly.
data_sources_used: dome live.html + daily_review.json (cited as 2026-04-17, not re-fetched this review)
dome methodology.json (verbatim 'display ratio is bookkeeping' quote; verified verbatim in c2 SEC-timestamp review this week)
dome formula_runtime.json (FORM-V51-001..006 claim not verified this review — flagged)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The section is our own prose, not a dome-side adaptive-tolerance argument. The counterpoint paragraph correctly acknowledges the dome's methodology.json concession rather than pretending it doesn't exist.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Four WIN-id citations in Group B do NOT match our own wins.json: WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076 (our registry tops out at WIN-069, 71 total entries including -b variants), and OPEN-007 is a dome-side Open Problem ID, not a wins.json entry. These are references to the dome author's internal monitor.py 'source' field markers (e.g., 'NASA JPL Horizons (OPEN-007 static win)'), not pointers to our review's WIN pages. The section does not clarify this for the reader. Severity: moderate — a reader searching our site for WIN-075 will find nothing.

holes_found.description: Group B WIN-id citations don't resolve in our own wins.json. The Group B table lists 'Prior WIN' column entries for WIN-073 (row 28, GPS Clock Offset Lorentz), WIN-075 (row 18, Ionospheric D-layer live), WIN-076 (row 20, Mascon Gravity Anomaly live), and OPEN-007 (row 26, Moon Angular Diameter Variation). Our data/wins.json has 71 entries maxing out at WIN-069. WIN-073/075/076 do NOT exist in our registry. OPEN-007 is the dome author's own Open-Problem ID (confirmed in our own SEC-6.5 globe-fingerprint analysis and EXP-176 notes), not a wins.json entry. A reader checking these references on our site will find nothing. A dome defender can charge: 'Your own audit cites WIN-073/075/076 as if they exist — they don't. How can I trust your 28-domain classification when 3 of the 14 static re-references point at WIN IDs you haven't even onboarded?' The section should either (a) clarify in-place that these numbers are the dome author's internal monitor.py 'source' field markers (e.g., 'source: NASA JPL Horizons (OPEN-007 static win)'), not links to our review, or (b) drop the WIN-NNN labels for the four unreviewed-in-our-registry rows and describe them by content only.
holes_found.recommendation: Two clean fixes: (1) Add one clarifying sentence above the Group B table: 'Prior-WIN column values are the monitor.py source-field labels the dome author uses internally to mark each cell as a static re-reference; WIN-NNN numbers above WIN-069 correspond to dome-side domains we have not yet onboarded to our review.' (2) Or, rename the column 'Dome source label' to avoid implying these link to our WIN pages. Option 1 is lower-cost and preserves the audit trail. Either closes the hole.

holes_found.description: Group count arithmetic does not add up. The section claims 'Group A (14 of 28) + Group B (14 of 28) = 28', then introduces 'Of the remaining cells: Group C (5 standard-model imports), Group D (2 framework recastings), Group E (3 loose checks)' = 10 more. 14+14+5+2+3 = 38, but there are 28 dashboard cells. Either (a) the groups overlap (Equatorial Gravity appears in both Group A row 4 and Group C row 4; GPS Clock Offset Lorentz appears in both Group B row 28 and Group D row 1), in which case the 'of 28' labels on each group header are misleading because they don't partition, or (b) Groups A/B don't actually sum to 28 and the 'remaining' framing makes sense. The section uses both framings without reconciling them. A careful reader (especially a dome defender) will catch the 38-vs-28 discrepancy and use it to charge double-counting.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one framing and commit. Cleanest option: reframe the groups as overlapping classifications rather than a partition. Replace 'Group A (14 of 28)' headers with 'Group A: 14 cells whose predictions share the λg=8,619 km / H₀=8,537 km fit.' Replace Bottom Line claim 'Of the remaining cells' with 'Overlapping classifications: X cells are also...' Alternatively, renumber Groups A and B so they partition 28 disjointly (some judgement calls on where Equatorial Gravity and GPS Clock Lorentz fit best) and present C/D/E as subsets of those. Either works; the current text is internally contradictory.

holes_found.description: The 'one ruler' analogy understates the number of fitted constants. Row 2 of Group A (Tesla Longitudinal Freq) lists shared constants as 'λg, va'. va = 1.574c = πc/2 is an independent constant derived from Tesla's 0.08484 s spherical-cavity measurement, NOT from the λg fit. So the 14 λg-dependent domains actually share at least two independent fitted constants (λg/H₀ pair and va), not one. The section's closing analogy — 'the dome model's λg = 8,619 km is the ruler. It was fitted once... Fourteen of the twenty-eight scored domains use it' — is softened by this: 13 use λg (or λg+H₀), 1 uses va. The core critique is still valid (same handful of knobs, few degrees of freedom), but 'ONE ruler' is an overstatement that a capable defender will catch.
holes_found.recommendation: Rephrase the Bottom Line analogy to 'The dome's handful of fitted constants — λg = 8,619 km, H₀ = 8,537 km, va = 1.574c, κ = 1.67 — each get re-used across many dashboard cells; λg alone appears in 13 of the 14 Group A domains, va in Tesla's frequency row, and so on. Fourteen labels cannot carry more independent information than the 4–10 knobs they are derived from.' This is factually tighter and matches formula_runtime.json's '6 formulas + 3 master constants ≈ 10 independent knobs' framing used earlier in the section.

holes_found.description: Group C table has 6 rows but the Bottom Line and TLDR both say '5 import standard-model or globe-model values.' Group C rows: K1 Tidal (Sidereal Day), S2 Solar Tidal, M2 Tidal, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift Scale λA, P-wave Shadow Zone. The off-by-one is likely because Equatorial Gravity is double-classified with Group A row 4, so it is counted there and not here. But the Group C table shows it as its own row.
holes_found.recommendation: Consistency fix: either drop the Equatorial Gravity row from Group C (cross-reference to Group A row 4 suffices) and keep the '5' label, or change TLDR/Bottom Line to '6' and mark Equatorial Gravity in Group C as '(also Group A #4)'. The clean fix is the former — drop the row with a one-line cross-ref.

holes_found.description: Tesla Longitudinal Frequency explanation (Group A row 2) says '~1.57c, a known superluminal phase velocity in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide.' The dome cavity waveguide model (Schumann TM0 mode) actually has phase velocity slightly below c, not above. The πc/2 ≈ 1.57c phase velocity comes from Tesla's spherical cavity resonator interpretation of the Earth, which WIN-001 correctly characterizes as 'the phase velocity of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth's radius.' These are technically different waveguide physics; 'Earth-ionosphere waveguide' is a different model (TM0 between conducting Earth surface and ionosphere D-layer) with its own dispersion relation. A defender with basic RF expertise will catch this.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'a known superluminal phase velocity in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide' with 'the phase velocity πc/2 of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth's radius — a standard RF textbook result, not a dome-specific phenomenon' (matching WIN-001's framing). Low-effort one-sentence fix.

holes_found.description: formula_runtime.json 'exactly six formulas (FORM-V51-001 through FORM-V51-006)' was not re-verified live this review. The raw-text archives (2026-04-07 and earlier) list constants and formulas by narrative but do not enumerate FORM-V51-00N identifiers. Live fetch would resolve; the c2 SEC-timestamp review verified methodology.json live but not formula_runtime.json.
holes_found.recommendation: Low-priority verification task: next curmudgeon run or analyst Mode-4 verify the six-formula enumeration via WebFetch against https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/formula_runtime.json . If not exactly six, update the section. If exactly six, add a '(verified 2026-04-NN)' footnote.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's dashboard ships TWO different σ values for the same aggregate. The static HTML audit box on live.html states '9.2σ' and 'Aggregate p-Value: 1.2e-20' (reproduced in raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 31–37). The dynamic status_history.json snapshot in the same archive (line ~178) states 'CROSS-DOMAIN σ: 14.26 across 19 domains (Fisher's method).' Two different σ values for the same statistic, in the same document, at the same time. Our Section 4.1 mentions only the 9.2σ headline. Adding a sentence — 'the dashboard's own data file (status_history.json) reports 14.26σ across 19 domains using Fisher's method while the static HTML box reports 9.2σ across 28 — the same statistic computed on different domain counts, producing numbers 55% apart, which is itself evidence that the aggregate figure is not a stable measurement' — turns an already strong argument into a self-refuting one. The dome's own inconsistency does the work.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 177-178 (DYNAMIC DATA SNAPSHOT claiming 14.26σ across 19)
Live verification via WebFetch to live.html and status_history.json would confirm the discrepancy persists in April 2026

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own Schumann derivation gives a DIFFERENT H₀ than the dashboard's constants box uses. raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 53 states: 'Derivation: f = c/(4H₀) → H₀ = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km.' But the constants box on the same page (line 21) shows 'H₀ = 8,537 km (Firmament height at pole).' Solving c/(4×7.83) = H₀ gives 9,572 km; the dashboard then uses 8,537 km (a 12% mismatch) in all other formulas. Either the Schumann derivation doesn't actually produce H₀ (it's a post-hoc match via a different formula), or H₀ was fitted elsewhere and the Schumann formula is inconsistent. Our section's argument that 'H₀ was fitted once to reconcile three measurements' has a stronger version: 'the dome's own Schumann derivation gives H₀ = 9,572 km, which is 12% away from the dashboard's published H₀ = 8,537 km. The derivation is decorative, not generative.' This would add a direct self-contradiction argument the current section omits.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 53 (Schumann derivation: H₀ = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km)
Section 4.1's existing paragraph on H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) 'reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements' provides the hook

stronger_arguments.description: NMP Drift Rate's adaptive tolerance history deserves a dedicated callout. The current section's Group A row 3 says 'predicted 22.2 km/yr, observed 37 km/yr — miss of 39.9% within a 50% tolerance window.' The dome's own archive note (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 267) is more damning: 'Note: Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed.' Calling this out as 'the tolerance was widened AFTER the prediction missed, turning a failed prediction into a passing one at the display layer' converts a 39.9% miss into direct evidence of tolerance-gaming. Section 3 (or the kill-shots tab) already covers this pattern, but Section 4.1 should explicitly flag NMP Drift as an example because it is sitting right there in the dashboard's own metadata.
sources: data/wins.json NMP entries for historical tolerance values
Part 3 or Part 2b tolerance-gaming analysis for cross-link

kernel_of_truth.description: The section correctly and importantly updates the old 20-domain analysis to the 28-domain reality. The core analytical move — identifying that 14 of 28 are mechanical re-reads of prior WINs that cannot fail, and another 14 share a single (or small) fitted-constant family — is structurally sound and matches the dome's own monitor.py source-field markers ('static win'). The cross-reference to SEC-2b.5 and the verbatim methodology.json 'display ratio is bookkeeping' quote give the section legitimate grounding in the dome's own published documents. The statistical-independence argument (28 labels cannot carry more independent information than 10 knobs) is the correct framework for critiquing the 9.2σ headline. The April 2026 expansion of monitor from 20 to 28 domains, plus the publication of formula_runtime.json and methodology.json, also represents a real improvement in the dome site's rigor and transparency — and the section credits this in its kernel-of-truth acknowledgment of EXP-176.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is our own prose. The equivalent here: the replacement block is materially stronger than the pre-EXP-176 version, but carries six identifiable residual issues (one major: the WIN-073/075/076 phantom-ID problem; two moderate: the group-count arithmetic and the 'one ruler vs multiple knobs' overstatement; three minor: the Group C off-by-one, the Tesla waveguide vs cavity-resonator imprecision, and the unverified formula_runtime.json count). The section is publishable in its current form but will be stronger with one major edit (phantom-ID clarification) and one or two additions (9.2σ-vs-14.26σ contradiction; Schumann-derived-H₀ vs dashboard-H₀ contradiction). None of the residual issues change the verdict; they refine the argument.

text_fingerprint.verdict: 28-domain classification published; minor citation and count-arithmetic cleanups pending

deeper_analysis:
The c3 review assesses the full EXP-176 replacement block integrated into Part 4.1 on 2026-04-17. The pre-EXP-176 version was a 1,724-word '9.2-Sigma Dashboard — One Constant, Not Twenty Tests' argument; the replacement is a 2,376-word '28-Domain / 100% Dashboard — One Ruler, Many Rooms' audit with five classification tables. The replacement's core analytical move is sound and well-grounded in the dome's own source files. Three concerns, described in holes_found, should be addressed before a dome-side defender turns them into talking points.

Taking the other side of the argument (per user preference 'think about the other side too'): the strongest dome-defender reply to Section 4.1 as currently written is a chained one. (1) 'Your own section cites WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076 as prior WINs — those don't exist in your review. Either you're fabricating citations or you haven't done the onboarding work you claim.' (2) 'Your Group counts 14+14+5+2+3 = 38, but we only have 28 cells. Your own classification double-counts.' (3) 'You call it One Ruler but your own Group A table shows Tesla using va = 1.574c, a separate fitted constant. So by your own admission it's multiple rulers.' (4) 'You quote methodology.json saying bookkeeping is not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit — that's my own caveat. You're using my own words to claim I haven't done what I openly said I haven't done yet.' Replies 1–3 are the holes_found items; reply 4 is already handled in the section's counterpoint framing and kernel-of-truth acknowledgment.

A capable dome defender can use replies 1–3 to charge 'sloppy audit' without ever engaging the substantive statistical-independence argument. Fixing all three (which are small edits, not structural changes) neutralizes that vector.

Two stronger arguments are left on the table: (a) the dome dashboard ships two different σ values (9.2σ in the static HTML audit box, 14.26σ in the dynamic status_history.json) for the same aggregate; (b) the dome's own Schumann derivation f = c/(4H₀) → H₀ = 9,572 km contradicts the dashboard's published H₀ = 8,537 km by 12%. Both are direct self-contradictions in the dome's own documentation; both are documented in the section's own raw-text archive; neither is surfaced by the current block.

Three WIN-id references (WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076) cannot be resolved in our data/wins.json, which tops out at WIN-069 (71 entries total with -b variants). These are the dome author's monitor.py internal source-field markers for static-win cells, not pointers to our review pages. The section does not disambiguate this for the reader. Fix: one clarifying sentence above Group B, or rename the column 'Dome source label'.

The group count arithmetic (14+14 claimed as partitioning 28, plus 5+2+3=10 'remaining') is internally contradictory. Fix: reframe as overlapping classifications or renumber so A∪B disjointly cover 28.

The 'one ruler' analogy overstates: Tesla's va is a separate fitted constant independent of λg. Fix: 'handful of fitted constants' is both truer and consistent with the earlier '≈10 knobs' framing.

Group C has 6 rows but Bottom Line / TLDR say 5. Fix: drop Equatorial Gravity row from Group C (cross-ref to Group A #4) or change the count labels.

The Tesla 1.57c 'Earth-ionosphere waveguide' phrasing is imprecise vs WIN-001's 'spherical cavity resonator.' Fix: one-sentence edit.

formula_runtime.json six-formula claim is unverified live this review. Fix: verify next run, add '(verified 2026-04-NN)' footnote.

None of these change the section's verdict. Recommended action: minor_edit. The block is materially stronger than c2's pre-EXP-176 text and, with a small cluster of targeted edits (estimated 4–6 one-to-two-sentence changes), becomes a clean publishable 28-domain audit.
